name: cpu_int_single
title: CPU integer, single core
short_description: Single core integer arithmetic throughput on one busy thread.

structured_text.description: Runs a dense stream of integer add operations on one thread using the widest vector unit the processor supports. Many independent add chains run at once so the integer execution ports stay saturated, and the working set stays in registers so the score reflects raw integer throughput rather than memory or cache behaviour.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Throughput is the number of integer operations completed per second, divided by one million, reported in Mops/s.
structured_text.value_interpretation: A higher number means a faster single core. This is the figure that matters for work that cannot be spread across many cores, such as a single browser tab or one game thread.
structured_text.reasoning: Single core integer speed is the clearest signal of how snappy a machine feels for everyday, latency sensitive work, so it anchors the synthetic CPU set.

domain_gaming.explanation: Game logic and the main update thread lean heavily on single core integer speed.

domain_office.explanation: Document and browser responsiveness is bound by how fast one core handles short bursts of work.

domain_software_development.explanation: Many build and tooling steps run on one core, and editor responsiveness depends on it.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Interactive edits dispatch on a single thread before any GPU work begins.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Timeline scrubbing and effect setup run on one core ahead of multi core export.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Scene setup and per object preparation run single threaded before the render fans out.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Real time audio graphs run on one thread and are very sensitive to per core speed.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Model editing and geometry updates run on a single thread between solver runs.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Serial sections and setup code gate otherwise parallel simulations.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Training is dominated by the accelerator, with limited single core influence on the host.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Tokenisation and request handling run on one core around the heavy compute.

domain_data_science.explanation: Interactive query setup and scripting run single threaded before parallel kernels start.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Per request latency depends on how fast a single core handles one unit of work.
